announcements.title: SAP Inside Track Belgium 2025
announcements.content:
Let's re-connect and share our knowledge with a fresh pint of beer or soda!
We welcome you as from 09:00 CET in Van Der Valk Hotel in Mechelen.
Sessions will run until 16:00 CET, followed by a wrap up and a drink.

announcements.title: Call for speakers open
announcements.content: We are currently accepting session proposals at: https://community.sap.com/t5/brussels-blog-posts/call-for-speakers-sap-inside-track-belgium-2025/ba-p/14088851

announcements.title: Pre-event: Codejam
announcements.content: Prior to the SITBe, a Codejam will take place, also in Van Der Valk Hotel in Mechelen. Stay tuned for more info. Special guest: André Fischer

sessions.name: Welcome
sessions.description: Breakfast!

sessions.name: Keynote
sessions.speaker: SITBE Team
sessions.description: Welcome back to our Yearly SITBE!

sessions.name: From Legacy to BTP: How Mambo Supply Brings Supply Chain to Every Cente
sessions.speaker: Amista
sessions.description: Mambo Supply is a set of supply chain applicatiosn for managing ordering and inventory in local centers. Originally built outside of SAP with limited integration, it has now been re-engineered on SAP BTP as an extensible solution connected to ECC. The new version delivers desktop and mobile access, while paving the way towards S/4HANA.

sessions.name: Clean Code is not Enough
sessions.speaker: Thibault Vandevoorde
sessions.description: SAP has been driving the message: keep the core clean, write clean code, and embrace the cloud with frameworks like RAP. But beneath the surface, one question remains: where is the architecture? In this session, we'll take a step back and look at 15 years of ABAP development — from ECC and WebDynpro, to Gateway, and now RAP — and identify a recurring pattern: we keep putting business logic in the wrong place. Whether it was in report programs, DPC_EXT classes, or today's behavior implementations, the absence of layered design and separation of concerns is still costing us maintainability, testability, and clarity. We'll explore what clean architecture means in an ABAP context, how to structure your code into external, integration, business, technical, and physical layers, and how to apply these principles even within the constraints of modern frameworks like RAP. If you've ever written a behavior class that feels like a procedural monolith, this talk is for you.

sessions.name: Integration Modernization with SAP Integration Suite
sessions.speaker: Jeroen Knops
sessions.description: After multiple PO to Integration Suite migrations I want to share our experiences on modernizing your Integration landscape. I will present the highlights and lowlights from a practical perspective

sessions.name: Between AI and API: Real-time integration for LLM applications using MCP
sessions.speaker: Vadim Klimov
sessions.description: In this session, we are going to explore how to infuse data from external sources into LLM applications on demand and in real time using a standardized mechanism based on the Model Context Protocol (MCP). I will demonstrate this approach through practical scenarios using SAP Integration Suite. In addition, I will also discuss common risks associated with MCP servers, along with best practices to consider and anti-patterns to avoid.

sessions.name: ABAP Cloud in Action
sessions.speaker: Andre Fischer
sessions.description: ABAP Cloud is the powerful development model for building cloud applications on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) and SAP S/4HANA, in the cloud and on premise. Experience live demos of the major ABAP Cloud capabilities around the ABAP RESTful application programming model which can be used to build and extend transactional and analytical applications.

sessions.name: ABAP Development with modern tooling
sessions.speaker: Pieter Lemaire & Matthis Vansteenhuyse
sessions.description: This session wil explore modern ABAP development, covering ABAP Development Tools in Eclipse, abapGit, documentation best practices (Markdown & Mermaid), and unit testing. We'll wrap up with an SFLIGHT demo, applying these tools in practice.

sessions.name: CAP & Joule: Extending beyond the First Build
sessions.speaker: Stijn Verdoodt
sessions.description: Generating a full blown CAP application in less than 20 minutes is definitely within the realm of possibilities of Joule. But how far can it help us with extending said app, and how closely will it be able to follow best-practice recommendations? In this session we will explore just that, generating an app from scratch and discovering how to implement key business logic in no time - all while sharing some valuable insights into the product as we go.

announcements.title: SAP Inside Track Belgium 2024
announcements.content:
Let's re-connect and share our knowledge with a fresh pint of beer or soda!
We welcome you as from 09:00 CET in the offices of ARHS.
Sessions will run until 16:00 CET, followed by a wrap up and a drink.

sessions.name: Welcome
sessions.description: Breakfast!

sessions.name: Keynote
sessions.speaker: SITBE Team
sessions.description: Welcome back to our Yearly SITBE!

sessions.name: Developer experience in SAP Cloud Integration: An alternative perspective
sessions.speaker: Vadim Klimov
sessions.description: In this session, we will explore some unconventional techniques and tools to support integration developers working with SAP Cloud Integration. To infuse a sense of old school, we will set aside popular browsers, code editors and IDEs, focusing instead on something distinctly different. These techniques will bring experience with SAP Cloud Integration to developers' fingertips in the most literal sense, not just figuratively. As a word of caution, be prepared for some nerd stuff.

sessions.name: Navigating the Tree: Unleashing Hierarchical Data in Fiori
sessions.speaker: Yor Rombaut, Fouad Walravens-Hjiyer & Stijn Puttemans
sessions.description: We will explore the possibilities offered by tree tables and analytical apps. We'll dissect the components that make up these applications, from back-end custom coding to front-end tree table interactions. Along the way, we'll compare Odata V2 with V4, covering essential aspects like searching, filtering, sorting, and editing. A live demo will showcase both back-end and front-end magic. Finally, we will share insights into the future of this technology.

sessions.name: UI5 Spreadsheet Importer V1 Release
sessions.speaker: Marian Zeis
sessions.description:
The UI5 Spreadsheet Importer has finally reached version 1 after a long beta phase.
In this session we will briefly describe the path of the last 2 years and how it went thanks to open source.
We will also show the latest features that have been added in the last few months.

sessions.name: SAP Build & Fusion Development
sessions.speaker: Jana de Klerk
sessions.description: SAP Build & fusion development. A lot of things have been written about it, mostly about combining citizen developers with pro-code developers. But the more interesting part is combining the Low-Code No-Code possibilities of SAP Build together with SAP Build Code. Let's take a look at the new possibilities and how to combine forces!

sessions.name: Business Technology Platform as a foundation for multitenant (partner) solutions
sessions.speaker: Geert-Jan Klaps
sessions.description:
In the first part of this session, I'll give you an overview of the different runtimes available in SAP Business Technology Platform and their advantages / disadvantages when it comes to building multitenant solutions.
In the second part of this session, we'll dive into the architecture and used services of a real-life example of a multitenant solution "Partner Portal" by TheValueChain.

sessions.name: Getting started with TypeScript in CAP workshop
sessions.speaker: Matthias Cobbaut
sessions.description: During this session we'll start a new sample CAP project with TypeScript in which we'll: - Add automatic type generation using @cap-js/cds-typer - Add a TypeScript Fiori Elements app - Add a local launchpad for testing our app using the cds-launchpad-plugin The session is aimed at developers with little to no experience with CAP or CAP developers who are interested in setting their first steps in using TypeScript.

sessions.name: Extending SAP S/4HANA with  blockchain through SAP BTP
sessions.speaker: Cas De Brouwer & Matthis Vansteenhuyse
sessions.description: What is blockchain; What are the use cases for blockchain; How did we use it in combination with SAP; Explaining blockchain technology

announcements.title: SAP Inside Track Belgium 2023
announcements.content:
Let's re-connect and share our knowledge with a fresh pint of beer or soda!
We welcome you as from 09:00 CET in the offices of Deloitte.
Sessions will run until 16:00 CET, followed by a wrap up and a drink.
If you're interested, there's also a codejam on 22th of September. You can make a complete trip out of it.

sessions.name: Welcome
sessions.description: Breakfast!

sessions.name: Keynote
sessions.speaker: SITBE Team
sessions.description: Welcome back to our Yearly SITBE!

sessions.name: Introduction into SAP Master Data Integration
sessions.speaker: Tom Rottiers & Pieter Janssens
sessions.description: This session will give an overview of SAP Master Data Integration and will demo some scenario's linked to SuccessFactors and S/4 Hana.  We will also explore some scenario's related to the extendibility and the possibilities of linking non-SAP applications to SAP Master Data Integration.

sessions.name: How to use native functionalities in SAP MDK using Swift
sessions.speaker: Robbe Wuyts & Wouter Lemaire
sessions.description: https://blogs.sap.com/2023/04/18/mdk-ios-nfc-uid-scanner-how-to-use-native-functionalities-in-sap-mdk-using-swift-and-debug-on-mac/

sessions.name: SAP Advanced Event Mesh: Event Broker Runtime at the Developer's Fingertips
sessions.speaker: Vadim Klimov
sessions.description: In this hands-on session, we will see how an event broker runtime that is a part of SAP Advanced Event Mesh, can be used by the integration developer community as the playground for exploring the capabilities of the broker and practising the development of event-driven integrations. No subscription costs, no high performance and powerful servers - throughout the session, we will use a developer machine to build a portable and disposable infrastructure, and then to deploy and run a sample scenario.

links.url: ./presentations/2023/sitbe 2023 - Vadim Klimov - SAP AEM - Event Broker Runtime.pdf

sessions.name: CAP Advanced Programming Model
sessions.speaker: Gert Mertens
sessions.description: We all love coding with the SAP CAP Framework (especially with TypeScript) since it speeds up our developments and adding extensions becomes a pleasure. This made us search for an approach to use cap in a scalable and reusable way and make our projects future and maintainable proof. This approach enables us with the following: organized CAP project structures, reusing code as much as possible, consuming remote services from different source systems, making it all typed using TypeScript!, clear separation between backend and UI.Consumption, typing and maintaining a nice overview of your CAP Project was never easier.

sessions.name: Early insights into the upcoming Edge Integration Cell runtime, as part of the SAP Integration Suite
sessions.speaker: Dimitri Sannen
sessions.description: Get early insights into the upcoming Edge Integration Cell runtime.

sessions.name: Fantastic CAP Gems & Where to Find them
sessions.speaker: Dries Van Vaerenbergh
sessions.description: With a lot of good content out there, developing CAP applications is fun! But when you have a hard time during development, finding the right info is essential. That’s why I want to share some “hidden” CAP Gems with you all and focus on one of my favorite CAP features called “Hybrid Testing”.

sessions.name: Creating the ultimate user experience with SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Mobile Start & SAP Task Center
sessions.speaker: Geert-Jan Klaps
sessions.description: Learn more on how to create the ultimate user experience for your end-users. Starting with SAP Build Work Zone, working up to a full blown mobile experience with SAP Mobile Start & SAP Task Center. Insights into the possibilities of SAP Mobile Start & SAP Task Center, based on a demo case leveraging a custom build application on top of SAP CAP 7 combined with SAP Build Process Automation.

sessions.name: Using RAP Managed scenario for “Brownfield applications”
sessions.speaker: Yor Rombaut
sessions.description: For a "brownfield" application that uses BAPIs, standard classes and tables in the back-end, RAP unmanaged is recommended. By using a small intermediary, you can also use the power of RAP managed in these scenarios, including all features and best practices of the RAP framework (CDS, actions/validations/determinations, auto CrUD, etc..). This session covers the architecture of the solution, the benefits and a customer reference case.

sessions.name: Latest innovations in SBPA and lessons learned while migrating from iRPA & Workflow management
sessions.speaker: Jana de Klerk
sessions.description: During this session, we will reveal the latest innovations in SAP Build Process automation and explore the challenges faced when migrating from SAP iRPA and Workflow management to SAP Build Process Automation, uncovering the gaps between the two. Throughout this session, we will share valuable insights and real-world experiences gained from organizations that have successfully made the transition from iRPA and workflow systems to SAP Build Process Automation. We will discuss best practices, key considerations, and important lessons learned along the way.

sessions.name: Introduction to CAP with PostgreSQL
sessions.speaker: Matthijs Mennens
sessions.description: This session will cover the setup of SAP CAP with PostgreSQL and additional examples

sessions.name: Next-Gen UI5 Control Development
sessions.speaker: Wouter Lemaire
sessions.description: Controls are one of the most important aspects of the UI5 framework. Over time the framework evolved to keep track of new browser versions and web technologies in general. Compared to other frameworks, UI5 is still the first one of its kind which is quite unique these days. The evolution of the UI5 framework has an impact on the rendering of the controls as this is key to the performance of the framework.UI5 Controls already evolved with the renderer version 2. Now, with WebComponents making its way into the framework, it changes the game again. With a more native web technology the rendering of the controls will take your UI5 app to the next level!Allow Peter and myself to introduce you into building UI5 controls ready for whatever comes next in the future of the ui5 framework and beyond!

sessions.name: Modern JavaScript and UI5
sessions.speaker: Mike Zaschka
sessions.description: Over the years, JavaScript has evolved significantly, while UI5 and its best practices simply did not keep up. In this session, we will look at the importance of using and ways to apply modern JavaScript to any UI5 app. And to support my claims, there will be memes.

sessions.name: Me (the developer) and AI
sessions.speaker: Jérémy Coppey
sessions.description: Still seeing in what it evolves but I will give a session about SAP developments (ABAP/UI5/...) and the benefits/drawback of AI

sessions.name: TypeScript: What's in it for me?
sessions.speaker: Stijn Puttemans & Jules Decock
sessions.description: Why use Typescript as best practice for ui5 projects: short introduction to typescript, what are the advantages and benefits, some lessons learned, how to get started.

announcements.title: The Big Re-connect
announcements.content:
2 years of homework, but we're back. Let's re-connect and share our knowledge with a fresh pint of beer or soda!
We welcome you as from 09:00 CET in the offices of TheValueChain.
Sessions will run until 16:00 CET, followed by a wrap up and a drink.
If you're interested, there's also a codejam on 23rd of September. You can make a complete trip out of it.

sessions.name: Welcome
sessions.description: Breakfast!

sessions.name: Keynote
sessions.speaker: Wouter Lemaire & Tom Van Doorslaer
sessions.description: Welcome back to our SITBe. After a 3 year break, we're back!

sessions.name: A harmonized UX with the BTP Launchpad Service
sessions.speaker: Stijn Puttemans, Yor Rombaut & Wouter Lemaire
sessions.description:
At Elia Group we are working on one of the biggest projects in Elia's history, called OneSAP. In this project the full SAP team is bringing all SAP systems from 50Hertz and Elia together as well as the functional as the technical parts of it. 
As part of this project we provide one harmonized User eXperience for all standard and custom Fiori apps from different SAP systems to our business using the Business Technology Platform Launchpad Service!
In this session we will share our strategy, architecture and migration steps to achieve this.

sessions.name: How the SAP Business Technology Platform takes you beyond Robotic Process Automation
sessions.speaker: Jana de Klerk
sessions.description: SAP Business Technology Platform has so much to offer when it comes to Hyperautomation

sessions.name: SEN Web Assistant, deep dive
sessions.speaker: Jérémy Coppey & Tom Van Der Beken
sessions.description: We will explain the benefits of SEN Web assistant, explain how to set it up, show its endless possibility's and make you want to implement this at your clients

sessions.name: Testing your CAP apps locally made easy with the open source cds-launchpad-plugin
sessions.speaker: Geert-Jan Klaps
sessions.description:
Tired of always creating and maintaining a local Fiori Launchpad Sandbox configuration in each CAP project manually?

Well look no further, with the open source cds-launchpad-plugin you can easilly generate a local Fiori Launchpad Sandbox out of the box!

In this session, you will get an overview of what's included in this plugin and what it's advantages are.

Furthermore, you will see how how you can incorporate this plugin in your own projects and how it allows you to easily test and demo your CAP-based Fiori / SAPUI5 apps and plugins hassle-free.

sessions.name: Decision Tree to keep the core clean in S/4Hana and S/4Hana Public Cloud Implementation
sessions.speaker: Sudip Ghosh
sessions.description: In this session i will talk about different strategy to keep the core clean in S/4Hana and S/4Hana public cloud imementation with use case example. In which scenario which strategy will work. Below is the blog where i have started talking about this. If you are architect , developer , customer or partner in sap consulting project this session would help you to make your decision strategy.

sessions.name: A Type Trip
sessions.speaker: Dries Van Vaerenbergh / Wouter Lemaire
sessions.description:
An End-to-End TypeScript flavored journey.
Building and testing a CAP TypeScript middleware (using the latest CAP-CLI-commands) with an SAPUI5 TypeScript app as cherry on top.

sessions.name: API Management
sessions.speaker: Ismail Kichaout
sessions.description: A high level overview about API Management solution

sessions.name: SAP BTP NEO to SAP BTP CF Migration Best Practices
sessions.speaker: Harish Yarra
sessions.description: In the session will talk about the process, tools and best practices for BTP NEO to BTP CF application migrations.

sessions.name: SAP Cloud ALM: monitoring Integration Suite and SuccessFactors
sessions.speaker: Tom Rottiers
sessions.description:
I would like to share our experiences with deploying Cloud ALM to monitor integration suite and SuccessFactors.
I am by no means an expert in Cloud ALM, but we have implemented it for some of our customers and I am eager to share our experiences.

sessions.name: SAP Cloud SDK - Speed up your nodeJs projects with Virtual-Data-Models and learn to love the benefits!
sessions.speaker: Cedric Heisel
sessions.description: Let me show you the benefits of the SAP Cloud SDK and how to work efficient with Virtual-Datal-Models (VDMs) in nodeJs typescript applications. I am going share some insights of a project, where we did release more then 40 VDM packages in private npm-feeds to build up a solid world of type-safe SDKs for integration purposes. See why it's worth it to spend to effort in setting it up and getting to know the SAP Cloud SDK and why it's a gamechanger in our digital transformation.

sessions.name: Event Driven Architecture with SAP Event Mesh
sessions.speaker: Sandesh Kurumella
sessions.description: With growing curiosity on event driven architectures at customers, I would like to introduce the basics of event based integrations and present a use-case how we have implemented 'SAP Event Mesh' at one of our customers to orchestrate an event driven architecture with SAP ECC.

sessions.name: OData V4 and SAP Cloud Application Programming Model
sessions.speaker: DJ Adams
sessions.description: In this session we'll look at what the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) has to offer for OData V4.

sessions.name: Best of Open Source SAP Community Projects
sessions.speaker: Marian Zeis
sessions.description:
Fortunately, thanks to the initiative of the SAP Commnuity Members, SAP is increasingly focusing on making essential components of the software open and public.
On this basis it was possible to create further components based on the open source code.
Just like the Inside Tracks, this SAP community open source software started as grassroots projects and have evolved to be indispensable for the daily development workflow.
This session will provide an current overview of the SAP community projects.
Projects within these categories are showcased:
- UI5
- ABAP
- CAP

sessions.name: CAP vs RAP
sessions.speaker: Aman Garg, Komal Bhadani, Sam M. Thenganalil
sessions.description: Thinking of creating a new application? What to choose? Why? This session would give answers to all these questions and much more!

announcements.title: March 15: Covid-19
announcements.content: Due to the Covid-19 measures, we are not certain yet if SITBe will proceed on the foreseen date of May the 9th. We don't want the brightest minds in the SAP ecosystem to fall victim to this nasty virus. We are keeping a close eye on the trends and government regulations to decide if we proceed on May 9, or postpone to a later date.

announcements.title: March 29: SITBe cancelled
announcements.content:
Sadly, the current crisis will not abate anytime soon and life as we know it will not resume for a while. Therefore, it's no big surprise that SITBe will not take place on the 9th of May.
We have decided to cancel the event until further notice, but we will be back in the future.
Stay safe, respect the rules imposed by your government and keep physical distance.

sessions.name: FaceRecognition with SCP
sessions.speaker: Wouter Lemaire
sessions.description:
Over the years you get to know more and more people and of course you always like to greet them by name. From time to time it can happen that you can’t remember the name of someone you know… which can be awkward.
The same thing happens to me too, more and more lately… To avoid awkward situations, I’ve been thinking about a solution for this. I’ve started with an app to store all the people I know together with a picture. This should help me to find the name of someone I already knew. I would still need to search in the list, which is not easy when it becomes a big list and you don’t remember the name…
I needed to add something to the app that could provide me the name of a person by just taking a picture. This way, I don’t need to search through a big list and don’t have to know the name.
This is where I came up with the Face Recognition app! In this session I will show how this app works and how you can do face recognition with SCP yourself!

sessions.name: Cloud: The Final Frontier
sessions.speaker: Christian Drumm
sessions.description: Cloud: the final frontier. These are the endeavors of an ABAP developer. Its mission: to explore strange new cloud worlds. To seek out new platform and new programming models. To boldly go where no ABAP developer has gone before!

sessions.name: Write more solid state code with functional programming techniques
sessions.description: Functional programming techniques can help you write code that is less prone to bugs, because it has a smaller surface area and follows principles that mean that the code "doesn't move" - it just "is". This session will give you an introduction to some of the fundamentals using JavaScript as the carrier language.
sessions.speaker: DJ Adams

sessions.name: Performance Measurement and Benchmarking of Custom Code in SAP Cloud Platform Integration
sessions.speaker: Vadim Klimov
sessions.description: The session will showcase some techniques and tools that can be utilized when measuring performance of custom code that is embedded in integration scenarios deployed to SAP Cloud Platform Integration. In particular, we will start from a bird's eye view on performance measurement using stopwatch benchmarking, and then will put it under the microscope and detail to a level of microbenchmarking.

sessions.name: SAP Community at its best
sessions.speaker: Svea Becker
sessions.description: Everyone is somehow involved in a community e.g. a sports club, neighborhood, a fan club, etc. Why are you part of these communities? The SAP Community offers a wide range of involvement and engagement opportunities. What are the similarities to your private communities? Join this session and find out what the SAP Community is, how to get part of it and how to actively contribute. After all, bringing people together is what community is all about.

sessions.name: CAP and Cloud SDK at work
sessions.speaker: Vincent Weiss
sessions.description: Since August 2019 I have been working with CAP to build Applications and APIs and also leveraging the Cloud SDK from time to time. This session will share a bit of the lessons learned and how to work with it.

sessions.name: Cloud Foundry, Kyma, Kubernetes, Knative - What do I need?!
sessions.speaker: Max Streifeneder
sessions.description: Containers are no longer buzzwords, they are reality and part of most development activities today. Let's take a quick look at the differences, the advantages and disadvantages and see what SAP has to offer in this context.

sessions.name: SAP Security
sessions.speaker: Joris Van De Vis & Kris Wauters
sessions.description: Joris and Kris have uncovered yet a whole new load of SAP security issues that could seriously endanger your business operations. This is the stuff of nightmares.

sessions.name: Safety First
sessions.speaker: Tom Van Doorslaer
sessions.description:
I'm a complete dummy when it comes to security, but apparently there are even bigger dummies. I won't teach you how to hack, because I have no idea myself. Instead, I'll show you how best intentions can lead to catastrophes.

This session will make you laugh, cry and feel embarassed about the silly way your IT landscape could be at risk. Promise me you'll never make such mistakes yourself.

sessions.name: Building a Trello Timesheet MTA on Cloud Foundry
sessions.speaker: Dries Van Vaerenbergh
sessions.description: Over the years the Trello application is a tool I’ve seen a lot of times. This combined with a customer that wants you to maintain your timesheet and an extra excel drove me crazy. This excel file had to contain the spent hours on a Trello card and all the details. But building an SAP Multi Target Application running on Cloud Foundry brought me relief. One single application to maintain your timesheet combined with the Trello cards (data) via the Trello API’s. No more timesheet and excel files to maintain. Happy Developers === Happy Customers 😎

sessions.name: Using the SAP Business Application Studio for UI development
sessions.speaker: Marius Obert
sessions.description: The SAP Business Application Studio, or short: App Studio, is the next generation of SAP's web-based development environment. The new architecture offers a new degree of freedom to the developer by adding support for VS Code plugins and access to a full-fledged terminal. In this session, Marius will show how the new App Studio simplifies UI development and why it is more than just a Web IDE 2.0.

sessions.name: Wrap-up, Reception and book Lottery
sessions.speaker: Wouter Lemaire & Tom Van Doorslaer
sessions.description: As usual, we'll be giving away free SAP press books. Don't worry, they are wrapped in sterile plastic and properly quarantained.

sessions.name: Cloud qualities out-of-the-box: Development and continuous delivery of high-quality enterprise apps on SAP Cloud Platform
sessions.speaker: Marco Dahms (SAP)
sessions.description: While running enterprise software in the cloud, we can decouple its lifecycle from that of the legacy enterprise systems maintained by the IT organizations residing on-premise. While the IT department is usually reluctant to enhance the functional scope of their legacy enterprise applications in order to reduce the risk of outages, we can provide new innovations to the business in a faster pace through the SAP Cloud Platform. The development of such cloud extensions shall be quick and efficient and lead to applications that meet high quality standards. In addition, business expects to receive new innovations instantly and frequently. To fulfill both expectations at the same time, you may use the S/4HANA Cloud SDK and its open-source Continuous Delivery Toolkit provided by SAP. In this talk, we will outline how this SDK simplifies the development process and helps to integrate new cloud solutions with legacy enterprise systems. We will also present a pre-configured, out-of-the-box delivery pipeline that requires minimal setup effort and ensures that the delivered software fulfills the expected quality criteria.

sessions.name: Hack the system
sessions.speaker: Joris Van De Vis
sessions.description: Ever wondered how secure your SAP systems are? They're not.

sessions.name: The Future of ABAP Programming
sessions.speaker: Paul Hardy
sessions.description: All that you know is at an end. This is in regard to both version management - CTS => abapGit and even the very nature of ABAP programming "the so called RESTful ABAP programming model" which SAP are working on. I am on the inside of both initiatives and want to share with you all the non-secret things I can...

sessions.name: Hitchhikers Guide to the Legacy
sessions.speaker: Laurens van Rijn
sessions.description: Handling legacy code

sessions.name: I Have Issues
sessions.speaker: Tom Van Doorslaer
sessions.description: You have issues, I have issues, we all have issues. But how can you keep track of these issues and manage your modern UI5 project?

sessions.name: Domain-driven design, ABAP and SAP Cloud Platform
sessions.speaker: Tudor Riscutia
sessions.description: Introduction to DDD: a bit of theory as well as how to apply these ideas in your day to day work as an SAP developer working on ABAP or SAP Cloud Platform.

sessions.name: Troubleshooting and debugging Node.js applications in SAP Cloud Platform Cloud Foundry
sessions.speaker: Vadim Klimov
sessions.description: The session will cover some practical hints and tips about increasing productivity of troubleshooting and particularly debugging Node.js applications that are deployed to SAP Cloud Platform Cloud Foundry. We will use classic options that require pausing application execution, as well as will see how verbose logging output can be collected dynamically for an application that is already running in debug mode. We will also apply described techniques to Node.js applications that involve source code transpiling - such as Node.js applications developed in TypeScript.

sessions.name: Hunting Zombies the SCP-Way
sessions.speaker: Christian Drumm
sessions.description: In my session I'll show how the SCP can be used to survive in a world after the zombie apocalypse.

sessions.name: Smart Coffee Machine – SAP Leonardo IoT Service
sessions.speaker: Dries Van Vaerenbergh & Wouter Lemaire
sessions.description: If you like to blow stuff up, this session really is for you. Come join us to learn how we gave a coffee machine some intelligence, and then blew it up.
